commands.review_summary:
The PR changes core reply dispatch to keep an owned reply operation usable after completion and threads routed thread ids into reply-payload dedupe and follow-up delivery with regression tests.

PR surface: Source +25, Tests +105. Total +130 across 9 files.

Reproducibility: Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue? Source inspection and the PR's new tests describe a credible reproduction path, but I did not run tests or see live Slack proof, so confidence is medium rather than high.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining action is contributor real-behavior proof and maintainer delivery-risk review, not a narrow automated code repair.

target.title: fix(reply): deliver final reply when queued follow-up claims session; scope dedupe to routed thread
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes a Slack (and cross-thread) **silent reply-drop** bug: a composed final reply starts dispatch but is never delivered, with **no failure signal** on the agent side — from the runtime's perspective the run "completes successfully" while the delivery layer drops the message. Observed orphan-reply rate climbed sharply (≈4% → ≈17%) and required users to DM to notice missing replies.

Two independent root causes, both in core reply dispatch:

### 1. Primary — final reply lost when a queued follow-up claims the session
`src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts`

`ensureDispatchReplyOperation` only treated the dispatch-owned operation as usable while `!dispatchReplyOperation.result`. When `runReplyAgent` completes the operation to drain queued follow-ups, and a second same-thread inbound then claims the session, the first final reply tries to **re-acquire the lane** (because `result` was set) instead of continuing with the payload it already owns. It deadlocks behind the queued work and never delivers.

**Fix:** continue with the owned operation whenever it exists (`if (dispatchReplyOperation)`), dropping the `&& !result` guard.

### 2. Secondary — reply dedupe collapses across distinct threads (#90044)
`src/auto-reply/reply/reply-payloads-dedupe.ts`

Reply dedupe compared only `originatingTo` (channel) without the routed thread, so a send in one Slack thread could suppress a later legitimate reply in a **different** thread.

**Fix:** thread the `originatingThreadId` through `buildReplyPayloads` (`agent-runner.ts`) and follow-up delivery (`followup-runner.ts`), and only fall back to channel-only matching when neither side has thread evidence. The new thread-aware guard is gated behind `!hasPluginThreadMatcher` so plugins that encode their own thread/topic identity (e.g. Telegram forum topics) keep using their existing matcher — preserving Telegram topic-dedupe behavior.

## Testing

- `tsgo` core + core-test: exit 0
- New/updated targeted vitest suites (fail-before / pass-after verified by reverting source while keeping tests — including a 120s deadlock-timeout repro that reproduces the orphan signature):
  - `dispatch-from-config.reply-dispatch.test.ts`
  - `followup-delivery.test.ts`
  - `agent-runner-payloads.test.ts`
  - `reply-payloads-dedupe` (84 tests)
- Broad sweep green: dispatch / freshness / reply-delivery / route-reply / threading / inbound-dedupe (82 tests)
- All Telegram topic-dedupe tests remain green after gating the new guard behind `!hasPluginThreadMatcher`.

## Notes

Diff is +133/-3 across 9 files (2 source fixes + thread-id plumbing + tests).
